concepts.name: MCP servers must register empty resources/list and prompts/list handlers
concepts.summary: Smithery's registry scanner probes every MCP server for resources/list and prompts/list regardless of declared capabilities; servers that only implement tools must register no-op handlers or the Python SDK returns JSON-RPC error -32601 (Method not found), which surfaces in Claude Code as 'Failed to list resources' warnings.
reasoning: Root cause: the -32601 error from Smithery comes from resources/list being invoked on a server that only registered list_tools and call_tool handlers.
Fix is to register empty list_resources and list_prompts handlers; this makes the Python SDK advertise both capabilities in the initialize response, which silences the Smithery probe cleanly.
Declaring capabilities alone is not sufficient — Smithery still probes both methods and expects a successful empty response, not -32601.
Applies uniformly to all four Golden Suite MCP servers (goldenpipe, goldencheck, goldenflow, goldenmatch); the same two-handler patch plus version/Dockerfile bump fixes them all.
